resources.name: 2026 New Mexico Veteran Health Care/Mental Health List
resources.description: VA Health Care Options for Veterans
resources.informationText:
The New Mexico VA Health Care System provides comprehensive primary, emergency, and specialty medical services to veterans across New Mexico, southern Colorado, and West Texas. The main hub is the Raymond G. Murphy VA Medical Center in Albuquerque, supported by 13 regional Community-Based Outpatient Clinics **Website:** https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/  

**Contact & Appointment Resources**
* **Main Appointment Line:** Call 505-265-1711 (Press 2) or use the toll-free Patient Response Center at 800-465-8262. 
* **24/7 Nurse Advice:** Call 505-265-1711 and press 3 to speak to a triage nurse immediately. 
* **Veterans Crisis Line:** Call 988 (then Press 1), text 838255, or chat online for immediate, confidential crisis intervention. 
* **Medical Transportation:** The New Mexico Department of Veterans Services provides free round-trip rides for medical appointments in rural counties via the Rural Veterans Transportation Program (call 505-429-5906) https://www.nm.gov/departments-and-agencies/department-of-veterans-services/

**Raymond G. Murphy VA Medical Center** 
* **Address:** 1501 San Pedro Drive SE, Albuquerque, NM 87108 
* **Phone:** 505-265-1711 
* **Website:** https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/locations/raymond-g-murphy-department-of-veterans-affairs-medical-center/  
* **Availability:** Emergency Department open 24/7; clinic appointments follow standard business hours. 
* **Key Services** include 50+ medical services, a 24-hour Emergency Room, a specialized Spinal Cord Injury Center, advanced surgery options, and psychiatric evaluations. 

**Regional Outpatient Clinics (CBOCs)** 
The VA operates clinics throughout the state to provide local primary care, laboratory testing, and extensive telehealth services:  
* **Northwest Metro VA Clinic:** Rio Rancho, NM https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/locations/northwest-metro-va-clinic/ 

**Specialized Care Programs** 
* **Mental Health Support:** Programs for PTSD, military sexual trauma (MST), substance use disorders, and transitional care for returning service members. https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/health-services/mental-health-care/
* **Women Veteran Care:** Comprehensive services including gynecology, maternity support, mammograms, and specialized mental health counseling. https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/health-services/women-veteran-care/
* **Caregiver Support:** Matching military caregivers with peer networks, local resources, and federal benefits. https://www.va.gov/new-mexico-health-care/health-services/caregiver-support/
* **State Veterans' Home:** The state also operates the New Mexico State Veterans' Home in Truth or Consequences, which delivers long-term nursing and specialized care. https://www.nmhealth.org/about/ofm/ltcf/nmsvh/

resources.name: 2026 Food Pantries for Low Income Seniors and Veterans in Bernalillo County
resources.informationText:
**Website**: https://www.bernco.gov/blog/2025/06/11/bernalillo-county-offers-free-groceries-to-low-income-seniors/  
**Phone:** For questions or more information, please call 505-314-0425. 
**Requirements:** Low-income seniors in Bernalillo County are invited to pick up free groceries each month at a series of regularly scheduled food pantries hosted in partnership with Silver Horizons. All veterans are welcome with proof of veteran status. Each participating household may attend up to two food pantries per month. Food is available while supplies last, and doors open 30 minutes prior to the scheduled start time. 

**Monthly Pantry Locations and Schedules:** 
**Raymond G. Sanchez Senior Center** 
9800 Fourth St. NW, 87114 
First Tuesday of every month 
10:30 a.m. – noon 

**County Extension Building**
1510 Menaul Blvd. NW, 87107 
Second Wednesday of every month 
12:30 – 2 p.m. 

**South Valley Multipurpose Senior Center** 
2008 Larrazolo Rd. SW, 87105 
Second Friday of every month 
12:30 – 2 p.m. 

**Tijeras Senior Center** 
10 Tijeras Ave., Tijeras, 87059 
Third Tuesday of every month 
10 – 11:30 a.m. 

**Paradise Hills Senior Center** 
6001 Paradise Blvd. NW, 87114 
Third Saturday of every month 
10 – 11:30 a.m. 

**Rio Bravo Senior Center** 
3910 Isleta Blvd. SW, 87105 
Fourth Wednesday of every month 
12:30 – 2 p.m.

**Seniors Only**
**ECHO Food Bank** (age 60+) 300 Menaul NW #226 (east side-- located in the back of the
shopping center with H&R block and Self Storage) 505-242-6777 (don’t need to call first)
--drive-through Mon-Thurs, 7:30 am – 4 pm (closed for lunch 12-12:30 pm)

**Home Delivered Meals for Homebound Seniors**
Seniors age 60+ who are unable to cook for themselves, have no one else to cook for them, and are unable to attend a meal site may be eligible to receive a home-delivered meal on a donation basis. Case Management Coordinators will conduct an eligibility assessment for this program. For more information about Home Delivered Meals, contact our Senior Information and Assistance Program at 505-764-6400.

**Veterans Only**
**Veterans Integration Center (VIC)** 505-296-0800 -  2701 Mulberry SE (turn south
off Gibson, west of University Blvd, just east of I-25)
--food pantry Friday 10 am-1 pm only for veterans, spouses and widows/widowers, National Guard and Reserves.

resources.name: GoodWill Opportunity Center
resources.description: Helps people needing housing support or those who are reentering the workforce, and people facing barriers in employment.
resources.informationText:
**What is offered:**
* free career fairs
* digital literacy classes
* youth employment programs
* wellness and life-skills classes
* a mobile app with online resources and access to career specialists.
**Eligibility:**
Homeless persons or near homeless veterans
Additional text:
**Main services:**
* Job placement assistance
* Job skills training
* Veteran’s services
* Community social services (partner organizations that can help with healthcare, housing, mental health and other needs).

resources.name: New Mexico Department of Veterans and Military Affairs (VMA) 2026
resources.informationText:
Veterans of the United States armed forces who reside in the state of New Mexico may be eligible for a broad range of federal and state benefits and services. One of the primary responsibilities of the New Mexico Department of Veterans Affairs is to connect veterans and service members with the benefits and resources available to them.  

**Website: https://www.dvs.nm.gov/** 

**Albuquerque VA Regional Office**
**Address:** 500 Gold Street SW, Albuquerque, NM 87102 
**Office Phone:** 505-346-4810 or 505-346-3986 
**Email:** nmdvs.info@dvs.nm.gov  

**For Assistance, call: 866-433-8387 OR if in Crisis - 988 (select 1)**

**Some of Our New Mexico Services** 

* Veteran Enrollment Assistance - 505-346-6562  
* Military ID to qualify for Services - 801 582 1565 X 2585  
* Health Care - Dental, Medical, Mental Health, Women’s Health https://www.dvs.nm.gov/healthcare-coordination/  
* Careers and Employment - https://www.dvs.nm.gov/field-services/  
* Legal Assistance - https://www.dvs.nm.gov/field-services/  
* Education and Training - https://www.dvs.nm.gov/veterans-education/  
* Housing Assistance - https://www.dvs.nm.gov/field-services/ 

**If you are a Veteran who is homeless or at risk for homelessness or need to connect with a Veterans outreach specialist, CALL:** 

**New Mexico Homeless Outreach Specialist- Therese Castello - 505-548-0585**             

The team will assist with your questions relating to homeless services and more.  

**OR call the National Call Center for Homeless Veterans at 877‐4AID‐VET (877‐424‐3838).  OR call 988 and press 1**   

**OR visit** https://www.va.gov/homeless/nationalcallcenter.asp or https://department.va.gov/homeless/what-services-does-va-provide-to-homeless-and-at-risk-veterans/  

**Need to speak with someone local?** 
To best serve Veterans where they live, our **VA-accredited Veterans Service Officers (VSO)** will help you with benefits assistance, claims, records, and more. Meet with a VSO, Face to Face! 
* Walk In Appointment: Every Thursday from 9:00 AM to 3:30 PM 
* Location: Albuquerque - 4801 Indian School Road, NE, Bldg 2, Suite 2  
* To schedule an appointment with a VSO see  https://www.nacvso.org/county-veterans-service-officers

resources.name: Gig & APP-based Work Options to Earn Extra Income
resources.informationText:
List of flexible work options you can use to earn money using your phone. These can be used alone or together with a job or staffing agency.
**1. DoorDash (Food Delivery)**
**What it is:** Deliver food to customers using a car, bike, or scooter. Best for flexible, ongoing income.
**How to get started:** Sign up at www.doordash.com or download the app, submit ID, background check, and basic info, and start working once approved
**Requirements:** Age 19+ in New Mexico, must have a Smartphone and Transportation (car, bike, or scooter)

**2. Bacon (Flexible Shift Work)**
**What it is:** App that connects you to local day labor and warehouse/event shifts.
**Best for:** Quick extra income, no resume needed
**How to get started:** Download the Bacon app, create a profile, apply for shifts near you, and show up to selected work locations to work
**Tips:** Check the app daily. Good ratings lead to more jobs.

**3. GigSmart (Get Gigs App)**
**What it is:** App for finding local temporary jobs like moving, labor, and events. Best for filling income gaps.
**How to get started:** Download 'GigSmart Get Gigs' app, create worker profile (basic info), apply for available shifts
**Tips:** Check multiple times per day. Works best alongside other jobs.

**4. Homeaglow (House Cleaning)**
**What it is:** Get paid to clean homes for clients. Best for reliable, steady side income.
**How to get started:** Apply online at www.homeaglow.com, complete background check, and accept cleaning jobs.
**Requirements:** Smartphone, transportation, and ability to clean homes
